quotes.verbatim_quote:
Jazz standards are the vehicles that jazz musicians use to improvise.
To become a better jazz musician, it is imperative that we study them
and explore their unique harmonic worlds. The more jazz standards you
explore, the more you will understand how to improvise over all of them.
quotes.why_load_bearing: This passage states the book's core pedagogical premise: that jazz standards are the primary medium through which improvisation is learned and developed.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
| chose them because they all con-
tain powerful harmonic and rhythmic lessons that apply to hundreds of
other standards.
quotes.why_load_bearing: This names the selection criterion — transferability of harmonic and rhythmic lessons — which frames the entire book's methodology.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
Indeed, music is a life-
time study. There is no final arrival point.
quotes.why_load_bearing: This is a rule-of-practice framing that orients the student's long-term relationship with the material beyond this single text.
